title: ETFs or Mutual Funds? How to Target Benefits of Both Worlds. | Dimensional
gist: Dimensional argues that choosing between ETFs and mutual funds is no longer an either/or decision, because ETF share classes can be added to existing mutual fund strategies, giving investors a single multi-class vehicle that combines benefits of both structures.

insights.claim: Investing in exchange-traded funds versus mutual funds is no longer an either/or choice for investors.
insights.speaker: Featuring Jake DeKinder, Head of Client Communications
insights.so_what: Advisors and investors can reconsider vehicle selection without trading off one structure's benefits against the other.
insights.quote: Is investing in exchange-traded funds or mutual funds an either/or choice? Not anymore.
insights.locator: In Episode 50 of The Informed Investor

insights.claim: Adding an ETF share class to a mutual fund strategy creates an additional access point to that same strategy.
insights.speaker: Featuring Jake DeKinder, Head of Client Communications
insights.so_what: Investors can access the same underlying strategy through whichever wrapper best fits their tax and liquidity needs.
insights.quote: ETF share classes provide an additional access point in mutual fund strategies.

insights.claim: Multi-class funds that include ETF share classes offer multiple potential benefits to investors versus single-structure vehicles.
insights.speaker: Featuring Jake DeKinder, Head of Client Communications
insights.so_what: Watch for asset managers rolling out ETF share classes on existing mutual funds as a growth and retention lever.
insights.quote: Multi-class funds with ETF share classes offer many potential benefits for investors.

source_meta.author: Featuring Jake DeKinder, Head of Client Communications
